_meta.description: Motor Insurance Risk-Based Premium System — ML Model Governance Record
_meta.author: IT22271600

dataset.name: DS2_Motor_Insurance_Sri_Lanka
dataset.label_strategy: Deterministic risk labels (not random) — no data leakage

risk_classifier.threshold_strategy: Cost-sensitive — minimises expected loss (FN cost = 3× FP cost)
notes: Had_Accident generated from deterministic risk function (not random)
Risk_Score computed from features (not random normal)
Premium predicts rate_pct (not raw premium) to avoid SI leakage
Market_Value_LKR and Sum_Insured_LKR excluded from risk features
risk_classifier.feature_importance_method: Permutation importance on 50-sample background set — normalized drop in prediction variance when feature is shuffled

rate_model.no_leakage_note: Market_Value_LKR and Sum_Insured_LKR excluded from features to prevent leakage

actuarial_config.pure_premium_formula: E[Loss] = P(claim) × E[severity | claim]  — both terms from DB, neither hardcoded
actuarial_config.expected_severity_source: AVG(claim_amount) FROM claims WHERE claim_amount > 0  [computed at runtime from insurance.db]
actuarial_config.frequency_source: Computed from claims/policies ratio in insurance.db
actuarial_config.base_frequency_source: COUNT(claims) / COUNT(policies)  [computed at runtime from insurance.db]
actuarial_config.severity_model_file: severity_model.json  [generated by compute_severity.py from live DB]

governance.explainability: Interventional SHAP (Janzing et al. 2020) — phi_i(x) = E[f(x_i, X_{-i})] - E[f(X)], vectorised over 50 background samples from policies table, implemented in shap_engine.py without external library dependency
governance.fairness_note: Gender excluded from rate model. Province loading capped.
governance.shap_reference: Janzing et al. (2020) 'Feature relevance quantification in explainable AI: A causality problem', AISTATS
governance.shap_timing_ms: ~11ms per prediction (20 features × 50 backgrounds, single batch)
